value.title: Study: Artificial Sweeteners May Trigger Blood Sugar Risks
value.content: Saccharin and other artificial sweeteners may raise blood sugar levels – a condition the sugar substitutes aim to help prevent – by altering digestive bacteria.

value.title: Do Artificial Sweeteners Raise Blood Sugar Levels?
value.content: No Direct Impact on Blood Sugar: Most artificial sweeteners do not raise blood glucose levels, making them a practical sugar substitute for

value.title: Artificial sweeteners and their implications in diabetes
value.content: by M Angelin · 2024 · Cited by 36 — ASs, now often used as alternatives to conventional sugars, may surprisingly influence blood sugar levels. In research conducted by Mathur et al
